candidate_overall.negative_finding: No Johnson-bounded candidate among C0-C3 restores the shape on every recorded row. C0 is Johnson-bounded but fails; C1, C2, and C3 restore the shape only while failing the Johnson size check.
candidate_overall.smallest_universal_but_unbounded_candida: distinct half-explanation codewords for f1 or f2 alone
candidate_overall.unbounded_selection_rule: Among candidates passing every recorded pair but failing the Johnson size check, record the one with smallest maximum per-pair count, then smallest total count, then C0/C1/C2/C3 order. This is not a bounded correction.

claim: Finite oracle-gated A.3 annulus MCA-from-CA insufficiency search: no Johnson-bounded cluster correction among C0-C3 restores the annulus shape on the recorded rows.

definition_sources.experimental/cap25_v13_experimental.tex:: prop:v13-tangent gives the exact r+1 tangent cell used as an oracle witness.
definition_sources.tex/cs25_cap_v12.tex:131: def:ca defines CA-bad slopes and eca normalization.
definition_sources.tex/cs25_cap_v12.tex:139: def:mca defines support-wise MCA-bad slopes and emca normalization.
definition_sources.tex/cs25_cap_v12.tex:4504: thm:deep-mca gives the r+1 oracle upper bound in 3r<=n-k.
definition_sources.tex/cs25_cap_v12.tex:4627: thm:johnson-list gives the interleaved Johnson list bound.
definition_sources.tex/cs25_cap_v12.tex:4939: thm:mca-from-ca gives the half-distance MCA-from-CA reduction.
definition_sources.tex/cs25_cap_v12.tex:4970: rem:half-scope identifies multiple explanations below half distance.
definition_sources.tex/cs25_cap_v12.tex:4974: cor:band-reduction records the support-mismatch reduction below half distance.

non_claims: This is not a proof of a corrected annulus MCA-from-CA theorem.
This is not a prob:band resolution.
C1, C2, and C3 restore the finite shape only by failing the Johnson size check.
The strict-annulus condition 2r>n-k is incompatible with n-2r>sqrt((k-1)n); the packet uses the a=n-r Johnson check.

row.strategy_difference_impossibility_reason: If 2r > n-k, then n-2r <= k-1, while sqrt((k-1)n) > k-1 for every k<n. Thus n-2r > sqrt((k-1)n) cannot hold in the strict annulus.

parent_finding.summary: A.3 as written has an unsatisfiable strict-annulus side condition, and the naive C0 pair-explanation cluster is insufficient under the natural a=n-r reading on (13,12,5,4).

theorem_problem_id: Task A.3 annulus bounded-cluster finite insufficiency search
